# Sebastian Peñaherrera

Sebastian Peñaherrera is a biotechnology engineer, researcher, and entrepreneur working on how cells communicate, coordinate, and make decisions.

He is currently focused on understanding bioelectricity as a key communication system for cellular intelligence, with particular interest in how electrical signaling shapes tissue-level behavior, regeneration, repair, and healthier human longevity.

Before this, his work centered on stem cell therapy, mitochondrial transfer, regenerative medicine, translational bioengineering, and precision health. He has conducted research on wound healing, senescence reduction, extracellular mitochondrial DNA, and long non-coding RNAs relevant to skin metabolism and regenerative therapies.

He served as Director’s Research Assistant at the Biomedical Institute at Universidad San Francisco de Quito (USFQ), where he worked on in vitro 3D culture systems that better mimic human physiology and improve the relevance of preclinical testing. He designed and tested a protocol that reduced senescence markers by 36% in induced senescent fibroblasts.

Sebastian has authored peer-reviewed publications in regenerative medicine and mitochondrial science, contributed to international scientific collaborations, and is co-owner of a submitted patent related to conditioned media that reduces senescence and enhances fibroblast proliferation.

He also founded a biotechnology startup, secured non-dilutive funding to advance R&D and early market entry, and built teams across science, operations, and product execution.

Recognition: TIME Fellow alumnus, Start Fellowship alumnus, IQ Conekta alumnus, and magna cum laude graduate of Universidad San Francisco de Quito.

Email: sebastian.penaherrera.c@gmail.com
Google Scholar: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=hfHKcFgAAAAJ&hl=en
